channel.title: Logistics on Dyson Sphere Program Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Logistics on Dyson Sphere Program Guide

item.title: Dyson Sphere Program Logistics Drones vs Belts: Complete Comparison
item.description:
DSP gives you two ways to move materials: belts and drones. Belts are local, simple, and fast. Drones are flexible, cross-planetary, and require zero physical infrastructure. Knowing which to use — and when — is one of the core strategic decisions in DSP factory design.

This guide breaks down throughput, use cases, cost, and scalability for each system.

TL;DR — Use belts for high-throughput local transport (within a planet). Use logistics drones for cross-planetary transport and flexible low-volume requests. Use vessels for massive interplanetary shipments of raw materials. For maximum efficiency, combine all three — belts for planet-level production, drones and vessels for network-level logistics.

Belt Transportation: When to Use It

Belt transport moves materials along physical paths. They're fast, reliable, and perfect for any situation where you're moving high volumes of materials between buildings on the same planet.

item.title: Dyson Sphere Program Mining Outposts: Coal, Fire Ice & Resource Veins
item.description:
Every DSP factory runs on raw materials — and raw materials live on planets that are rarely the planet where your main factory sits. Mining outposts are how you get coal from a gas giant's moon, titanium from an ice world, and fire ice from the dark reaches of the galaxy without leaving your main factory's logistics grid.

This guide covers how to build mining outposts, position mining machines correctly, and connect them to your logistics network so resources flow automatically.

item.title: ILS Not Moving Items? Here's the 30-Second Fix (DSP Guide)
item.description:
Your ILS has power. Ships are docked. Nothing moves.

Yeah. This is the #1 “why the hell isn't this working” question in DSP. Let's fix it in 30 seconds flat.

TL;DR — Two things to check:
1. One planet must be Supply, the other Demand (not both Supply)
2. Your vessels need Space Warpers to fly. Put some in the slot.

Supply vs Demand — The 1-Minute Show

Every ILS slot has a dropdown: Supply, Demand, and Store. Half the community doesn't know these make the difference between a dead ILS and a humming factory.
